title: Original Honoring Our WWII Merchant Mariners Act of 2025
short_title: Original Honoring Our WWII Merchant Mariners Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill gives money to World War II merchant marine veterans who never got GI Bill benefits after the war. Each veteran who qualifies can receive twenty five thousand dollars from the government. The bill sets aside one hundred twenty five million dollars total for these payments. Veterans must apply for the money within one year, and they must meet certain requirements about their service during the war.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Disability Assistance and Memorial Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Original Honoring Our WWII Merchant Mariners Act of 2025.

sections.title: Section 2  Payments to Individuals Who Served During World War II in the United States Merchant Marine
sections.text: This section creates a new compensation fund and sets up rules for payments to certain World War II veterans. The Secretary of Veterans Affairs must create a fund called the Merchant Mariner Equity Compensation Fund. Money from this fund will be used to pay eligible individuals who served in the merchant marine during World War II. To be eligible, a person must apply within one year of this law taking effect. They must not have already received benefits under the Servicemen's Readjustment Act of 1944 (also known as the GI Bill). They must have done qualified service between December seventh nineteen forty one and December thirty first nineteen forty six. Qualified service means the person was a crew member on a merchant marine vessel that was operated by the War Shipping Administration or Office of Defense Transportation. The vessel must have operated in waters outside of inland waters, the Great Lakes, and other lakes, bays, and harbors of the United States. The vessel must have been under contract with or owned by the United States Government and serving the Armed Forces. The person must have been properly licensed to work on such vessels by an authorized United States officer or employee. The Secretary can accept a DD-214 form (a military discharge document) as proof of this service. Each eligible person will receive one payment of twenty five thousand dollars. Payments will be made in the order applications are received. Congress authorizes one hundred twenty five million dollars for fiscal year 2026 for this fund. This money stays available until it is spent. The Secretary must report to Congress each year about how the fund is working, including how many people applied, how many received benefits, how much money was paid out, and estimates for future funding needs. The Secretary must create regulations to carry out this program within one hundred eighty days after this law takes effect.
